We see then to whom the word of wisdom belongs, and we believe it right to teach it in connexion with every other principle necessary for the perfection of the people of God ; while, on the other hand, an adherence to the word of wisdom in the neglect of the other commandments of the Lord, is like faith without works — it is dead, being alone, and will profit us nothing.

We see then to whom the word of wisdom belongs, and we believe it right to teach it in connexion with every other principle necessary for the perfection of the people of God ; while, on the other hand, an adherence to the word of wisdom in the neglect of the other commandments of the Lord, is like faith without works — it is dead, being alone, and will profit us nothing.

We see then to whom the word of wisdom belongs, and we believe it right to teach it in connexion with every other principle necessary for the perfection of the people of God ; while, on the other hand, an adherence to the word of wisdom in the neglect of the other commandments of the Lord, is like faith without works — it is dead, being alone, and will profit us nothing.
